channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-03
item.description:
Our community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects recently. In the scienceverse/metacheck project, Lakens has made significant strides in building out the datacheck capability, including the creation of initial modules and refinement of error handling. Meanwhile, giladfeldman/docpluck has released version v2.4.102 with important fixes, including the recovery of "2-for-minus" in Camelot HTML table cells and updates to documentation and changelogs. Notably, these projects highlight the importance of robust data handling and documentation, which are also relevant to the JamieCummins/regcheck project, where the removal of the sign-up feature on the staging branch may indicate a shift towards more streamlined user interactions. Overall, our community is celebrating a period of active development and collaboration, with connections between projects underscoring the value of open-source research tools. As we continue to build and refine these tools, we look forward to seeing the impact of this work on the broader research community.

40 commits across 5 active repositories this week.

Most active:

regcheck18 commits
docpluck12 commits
escicheck5 commits
metacheck3 commits
coarse2 commits

item.title: metacheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `data_check` branch was the focus of recent work, where the first version of datacheck modules was added, as stated in the commit message "first version of datacheck modules". On this same branch, a fix was made to "prevent double crossref api", and an additional change was made to "log errors", which enhances the robustness of the code. The `data_check` branch saw these specific changes to its datacheck modules and error handling. The work on the `data_check` branch appears to be foundational, with the initial module creation and subsequent refinement of its functionality. The commits suggest that the developer, Lakens, is building out the datacheck capability with attention to potential issues like API calls and error logging.

1 commit(s) today:

  • bd8c890 [data_check] first version of datacheck modules — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 5 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where a fix was applied to recover the "2-for-minus" in Camelot HTML table cells, specifically in the efendic B-column, and the version was bumped to v2.4.102. The documentation was updated to record a 2026-07-02 /api/extract unhandled-error incident in the lessons section. The documentation was also archived by removing resolved reconciliation handoffs and adding source findings and specifications. Additionally, the CHANGELOG was updated to version v2.4.101 and reconciliation findings were added to the release documentation. Fixes were applied to tables, including stripping LONG caption-tail prose headers and fixing the efendic T3 running-header and T2-5 caption-tail-prose strip, with a version bump to v2.4.101. Initial work-in-progress was done on tables to reconcile concurrent table-capture sessions onto greedy+rescue.

5 commit(s) today:

  • 54b7923 [main] fix(glyph): recover 2-for-minus in Camelot HTML table cells (efendic B-column) + v2.4.102 — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: coarse: 2 commits today
item.description:
Davidvandijcke/coarse

Work was done on the 'dev' and 'main' branches. On the 'dev' branch, the confirmation email and review page were localized to follow the review language, as stated in the commit message "fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244)". The 'dev' branch also had its schema_migrations bookkeeping table updated to enable RLS, according to the commit "fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243)". Additionally, Fable 5 was re-enabled in the model picker on both the 'dev' and 'main' branches, as seen in the commits "fix(web): re-enable Fable 5 in the model picker". The 'dev' branch was also updated with changes from the 'main' branch and the 'release/v1.8.0' branch through merge operations.

2 commit(s) today:

  • d93b6f8 [dev] fix(web): re-enable Fable 5 in the model picker — Davidvandijcke
  • d6df84a [main] fix(web): re-enable Fable 5 in the model picker — Davidvandijcke
  • 05aad76 [main] Merge pull request #245 from Davidvandijcke/dev — David Van Dijcke
  • 49a72b3 [dev] Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into sync/reconcile-v1.8.0 — Davidvandijcke
  • 2e86c5f [dev] fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244) — David Van Dijcke
  • 907c629 [dev] Merge pull request #242 from Davidvandijcke/release/v1.8.0 — David Van Dijcke
  • f54b584 [dev] fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243) — David Van Dijcke
  • ba19e15 [dev] chore(release): v1.8.0 — Davidvandijcke
  • 262305d [dev] docs(experiments): record PR-F language experiment (keep Design A) (#241) — David Van Dijcke
  • 2e1c796 [dev] feat(i18n): localize setup + compare pages; follow site language (#239) (#240) — David Van Dijcke
  • ef4f2eb [feat/i18n-setup-compare] feat(i18n): localize setup + compare pages; follow site language (#239) — Davidvandijcke
  • e8c3845 [dev] feat(i18n): cross-language review-consistency eval harness (PR-F) (#236) (#238) — David Van Dijcke
  • f1fbfe9 [dev] i18n(nl): Dutch site-UI wording refinements (#235) (#237) — David Van Dijcke
  • c3370c4 [feat/237-lang-eval] feat(i18n): cross-language review-consistency eval harness (PR-F) (#236) — Davidvandijcke
  • 228b694 [feat/nl-flemish] i18n(nl): Dutch site-UI wording refinements (#235) — Davidvandijcke
  • 1910472 [dev] feat(i18n): localize status + review pages; Opus QA of all 12 catalogs (#233) (#234) — David Van Dijcke
  • 9ac6256 [feat/i18n-status-review-pages] feat(i18n): localize status + review pages; Opus QA of all 12 catalogs (#233) — Davidvandijcke
  • 8faa90a [dev] feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H) (#231) (#232) — David Van Dijcke
  • 395a58c [feat/231-site-i18n] feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H) (#231) — Davidvandijcke
  • eae44c1 [dev] feat(i18n): localized review headers + completion email (PR-E) (#229) (#230) — David Van Dijcke

View all commits →
